subtasks.description:
Create src/widgets/Label.mag — a simple static text widget subclassing View.

First create the src/widgets/ directory if it does not exist.

File: src/widgets/Label.mag
Namespace: 'Alto', Import: 'Go'
Subclass View (not Object).
Instance vars: text style

Methods to implement:

1. classMethod: text: aString
   | instance |
   instance := super new.
   instance initialize.
   instance privateText: aString.
   ^instance

2. method: initialize
   super initialize.
   text := ''.
   style := nil

3. method: privateText: aString
   text := aString

4. method: displayOn: aCellBuffer
   Render text into aCellBuffer using putString: text x: 0 y: 0 style: style.
   Use integer char codes, NOT $x syntax.
   ALL temp vars declared at method top.

5. method: text: newText
   text := newText.
   self invalidate
   (invalidate is inherited from View — sets needsRedraw := true)

6. method: text
   ^text

7. method: style: aStyle
   style := aStyle.
   self invalidate

8. method: style
   ^style

Critical conventions (from past review failures):
- Use instanceVars: keyword form (NOT bracket-delimited class body)
- No mid-block | var | declarations — ALL temp vars at method top
- No ifNil:/ifNotNil: — use isNil ifTrue: / notNil ifTrue:
- Do NOT use Array>>add: or Array>>removeAt: — use copyWith:/reject: if needed
- Coordinates are 0-based in CellBuffer
- classMethod: new pattern: | instance | instance := super new. instance initialize. ^instance

Reference files for syntax patterns:
- src/mvc/View.mag (parent class)
- src/display/CellBuffer.mag (putString: str x: x y: y style: style)

Create src/widgets/ListView.mag — a scrollable selectable list widget subclassing View.

First create the src/widgets/ directory if it does not exist.

File: src/widgets/ListView.mag
Namespace: 'Alto', Import: 'Go'
Subclass View (not Object).
Instance vars: items selectedIndex scrollOffset onSelectBlock

Methods to implement:

1. classMethod: items: anArray onSelect: aBlock
   | instance |
   instance := super new.
   instance initialize.
   instance privateItems: anArray.
   instance privateOnSelect: aBlock.
   ^instance

2. method: initialize
   super initialize.
   items := #().
   selectedIndex := 0.
   scrollOffset := 0.
   onSelectBlock := nil

3. method: privateItems: anArray
   items := anArray

4. method: privateOnSelect: aBlock
   onSelectBlock := aBlock

5. method: displayOn: aCellBuffer
   Render visible items into the buffer. Key logic:
   - Determine visibleCount from buffer height (aCellBuffer height).
   - Loop from 0 to visibleCount - 1 (use 'i' as loop var).
   - For each i, compute itemIndex := scrollOffset + i.
   - If itemIndex < items size, get the item string.
   - If itemIndex = selectedIndex, use a reverse-video style (create a Dictionary with #attrs set to indicate reverse). Otherwise use nil style.
   - Call aCellBuffer putString: itemStr x: 0 y: i style: rowStyle.
   - ALL temp vars (visibleCount, i, itemIndex, itemStr, rowStyle) declared at method top.
   - For reverse video style, create a Dictionary: style := Dictionary new. style at: #attrs put: #reverse. This is the convention for CellBuffer styles.
   - Use 0-based coordinates.

6. method: handleEvent: evt
   Handle keyboard events for list navigation:
   - Declare all temp vars at method top (key).
   - Get key from event (evt at: #key or evt key — check how Controller.mag dispatches events; likely evt is a Dictionary or event object).
   - If key = #up (or 'Up' string): decrement selectedIndex. If selectedIndex < 0, set to 0. Adjust scrollOffset if selectedIndex < scrollOffset. Call self invalidate.
   - If key = #down (or 'Down' string): increment selectedIndex. If selectedIndex >= items size, set to items size - 1. Adjust scrollOffset if selectedIndex >= scrollOffset + visibleHeight. Call self invalidate.
   - If key = #enter (or 'Enter' string): if onSelectBlock notNil, call onSelectBlock value: (self selectedItem).
   - NOTE: Since the exact event format is not yet finalized, use simple key symbol comparison. The event is likely a Dictionary with a #key entry.
   - For scroll adjustment when moving down: check if buffer is notNil, get visible height from buffer height, then if selectedIndex >= (scrollOffset + buffer height), set scrollOffset := selectedIndex - buffer height + 1.
   - For scroll adjustment when moving up: if selectedIndex < scrollOffset, set scrollOffset := selectedIndex.

7. method: selectedItem
   ^items at: selectedIndex

8. method: items: newItems
   items := newItems.
   selectedIndex := 0.
   scrollOffset := 0.
   self invalidate

9. method: selectedIndex
   ^selectedIndex

10. method: items
    ^items

Critical conventions (from past review failures):
- Use instanceVars: keyword form (NOT bracket-delimited class body)
- No mid-block | var | declarations — ALL temp vars at method top
- No ifNil:/ifNotNil: — use isNil ifTrue: / notNil ifTrue:
- Do NOT use Array>>add: or Array>>removeAt: — use copyWith:/reject: if needed
- Coordinates are 0-based in CellBuffer
- Use integer char codes not $x syntax

Reference files for syntax patterns:
- src/mvc/View.mag (parent class — has invalidate, displayOn:, draw, bounds:, buffer)
- src/mvc/Controller.mag (handleEvent: pattern)
- src/display/CellBuffer.mag (putString: str x: x y: y style: style, width, height)

Create test/test_widgets.mag — comprehensive tests for Label and ListView widgets.

File: test/test_widgets.mag
Namespace: 'Alto', Import: 'Go'

Follow the test pattern from test/test_cellbuffer.mag and test/mvc_spec_test.mag:
- Create a WidgetTests class with classMethod: run that returns failed count.
- Use | passed failed | counters, increment on each assertion.
- Print 'FAIL: description' on failures.
- Print summary at end.

Tests for Label:
1. Label text: creates a Label with correct text — (label := Label text: 'Hello'. label text = 'Hello')
2. Label starts with needsRedraw true
3. Label displayOn: writes text to buffer — create a CellBuffer width: 10 height: 1, set label bounds to Rectangle matching, call label draw, check buffer cellAt: 0 y: 0 has char = 72 (ASCII 'H')
4. Label text: newText updates text and sets needsRedraw — set text:, check text getter returns new value, check needsRedraw = true
5. Label style: sets style — set style:, check getter
6. Label displayOn: with empty text does not crash

Tests for ListView:
1. ListView items:onSelect: creates with correct items — (lv := ListView items: #('a' 'b' 'c') onSelect: [nil]. lv items size = 3)
2. ListView selectedIndex starts at 0
3. ListView selectedItem returns first item
4. ListView displayOn: renders items — create CellBuffer width: 10 height: 3, set bounds, call draw, check buffer has chars from first item at y: 0
5. ListView displayOn: highlights selected item (check style at selected row)
6. ListView handleEvent: down arrow increments selectedIndex — send event Dictionary with #key -> #down, check selectedIndex = 1
7. ListView handleEvent: up arrow at 0 stays at 0 — send #up event, check selectedIndex = 0
8. ListView handleEvent: down does not exceed items size - 1
9. ListView handleEvent: enter triggers onSelectBlock — use a flag variable, set onSelectBlock to set flag, send #enter event, check flag
10. ListView items: newItems resets selectedIndex to 0 and invalidates
11. ListView scrolling: with 3-height buffer and 5 items, moving down to index 3 adjusts scrollOffset

IMPORTANT: ALL temp vars at method top. No mid-block declarations. Use isNil ifTrue: not ifNil:.

To run tests: mag -m 'WidgetTests.run' src/display/Rectangle.mag src/display/CellBuffer.mag src/mvc/Model.mag src/mvc/View.mag src/widgets/Label.mag src/widgets/ListView.mag test/test_widgets.mag

Note load order: Rectangle and CellBuffer must come before View, View before widgets, widgets before tests.

Review all Phase 4b widget files for correctness and convention compliance.

Files to review:
- src/widgets/Label.mag
- src/widgets/ListView.mag
- test/test_widgets.mag

Check against these known failure patterns from previous phases:
1. No mid-method temp var declarations (ALL | var | blocks at method top only)
2. No ifNil:/ifNotNil: — must use isNil ifTrue: / notNil ifTrue:
3. No Array>>add: or Array>>removeAt: — use copyWith:/reject:
4. instanceVars: keyword form (NOT bracket class body syntax)
5. Subclass View (not Object) for widgets
6. 0-based indexing for CellBuffer coordinates
7. Integer char codes not $x syntax
8. super initialize called in widget initialize methods
9. invalidate called when state changes (text:, items:)
10. displayOn: renders to the passed aCellBuffer, not to self buffer
11. Style dictionaries use at:put: not message-send accessors
12. No use of nonexistent methods (charCode — use value instead)
13. Correct string iteration (String>>at: is 0-based)
14. Guard against nil buffer in draw (inherited from View, but verify)

Also verify:
- Tests actually exercise the widget rendering path (set bounds before draw)
- Test load order is correct in the run command
- ListView scroll logic handles edge cases (empty list, single item)

Run tests if possible: mag -m 'WidgetTests.run' src/display/Rectangle.mag src/display/CellBuffer.mag src/mvc/Model.mag src/mvc/View.mag src/widgets/Label.mag src/widgets/ListView.mag test/test_widgets.mag

Report pass/fail with specific bug descriptions if any issues found.
